description: The Cuntrera-Caruana Mafia clan was a Sicilian Mafia clan originating from Siculiana, Sicily, founded in the 1950s. It held a key position in international drug trafficking and money laundering for Cosa Nostra during the 1980s and 1990s, with operations extending to Canada and Venezuela. The clan was described by Italian prosecutors as an 'international holding' providing drug-trafficking routes and money-laundering channels. It was considered defunct by the 2000s.

country_links.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Caruana preferred Rio de Janeiro in Brazil.
country_links.context: A member of the clan moved to Rio de Janeiro, establishing a presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Montreal is the first base outside Sicily for the Cuntrera-Caruana clan.
country_links.context: The clan established a base in Montreal and later operated drug trafficking and distribution networks in Canada.

country_links.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Cuffaro and Pasquale Caruana were arrested in Germany.
country_links.context: Members of the clan were arrested in Germany, indicating their presence in the country.

country_links.evidence_quote: Pasquale Cuntrera was arrested some days later in Fuengirola, Spain
country_links.context: A clan member was arrested in Spain while waiting to travel to Venezuela.

country_links.evidence_quote: the clan was picking up the heroin in Thailand since 1983.
country_links.context: The clan sourced heroin from Thailand, picking it up there since 1983.

country_links.evidence_quote: some went to the United Kingdom, to Woking, the stockbrokers-belt near London
country_links.context: Some clan members moved to Woking, near London, using it as a residence for drug trafficking.

country_links.evidence_quote: Venezuela became an important hideout. 'Venezuela has its own Cosa Nostra family as if it is Sicilian territory'...
country_links.context: The clan operated in Venezuela, using it as a hideout and base for cocaine trafficking.
